scope_description: Multi-branch Eastern European tree with historical place-name changes and jurisdiction traps: Brest-Litovsk/Brisk/Brest, Danzig/Gdańsk, Ekaterinoslav/Dnipropetrovsk/Dnipro, Hrubieszów/Koniuchy, and Molotschna/Molochansk.

input_dna_matches.expected_link: Mennonite colony adjacency; separate from Jewish Levitin branch
Molotschna/Khortitsa Mennonite cluster; not proof of direct Wiens ancestor

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth record for Freida, daughter of Yankel Friedman, Jewish community of Brisk/Brest-Litovsk.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms old jurisdiction for I7; modern Brest/Belarus is not the search jurisdiction for 1863.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of Tatiana/Tiena Scherbatenko, registered in Hrubieszów-area parish/civil district.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: routes 1902 Koniuchy to Congress Poland/Russian Empire rather than modern Lubelskie only.

input_archive_snippets.place: Dnipropetrovsk / former Ekaterinoslav region, Ukrainian SSR

input_archive_snippets.expected_use: corrects GEDCOM place mismatch: 1924 is Soviet-era context, not Russian Empire.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: All places normalized to modern countries: Brest Belarus, Gdańsk Poland, Dnipro Ukraine, Molotschna Ukraine, Koniuchy Poland.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: low-confidence import that loses historical jurisdictions; should trigger place normalization warning.

input_user_assertions.assertion: Because Freida was born in Brest, search modern Belarus civil records only.
Danzig is Germany, so use German archives for all periods.
Molotschna is just modern Ukraine; no separate Mennonite workflow needed.
1902 Koniuchy is Polish because it is in Poland today.

embedded_errors.reason: Brest 1863 should route to Brest-Litovsk/Brisk, Grodno Governorate, Russian Empire
Koniuchy 1902 should route to Congress Poland/Russian Empire
Ekaterinoslav label used for 1924; Soviet Dnipropetrovsk/Dnipro context required
1880 Danzig is German Empire/West Prussia; modern Gdańsk/Poland should not be search label
Mennonite Wiens match should not be merged into Jewish Levitin branch solely by regional adjacency
public tree normalized all places to modern countries and lost historical context

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse all place strings and extract event year, old name, modern name, language variant and candidate jurisdiction.
Step 2: Map Brest/Brisk/Brest-Litovsk: for 1863 use Grodno Governorate, Russian Empire; modern Belarus only as modern location field.
Step 3: Map Koniuchy/Hrubieszów: for 1902 use Congress Poland/Russian Empire with Hrubieszów-area routing.
Step 4: Map Ekaterinoslav/Dnipropetrovsk/Dnipro: for 1924 use Soviet-era Dnipropetrovsk context, not pre-1917 Ekaterinoslav Governorate.
Step 5: Map Danzig/Gdańsk: for 1880 use German Empire / West Prussia; for modern geocoding use Gdańsk, Poland.
Step 6: Map Molotschna/Molochansk/Halbstadt: for 1845 use Mennonite colony in Taurida Governorate, Russian Empire.
Step 7: Evaluate DNA clusters separately: Levitin/Friedman Jewish, Katz/Scherbatenko Hrubieszów, Mennonite Wiens/Friesen; do not merge populations by adjacency.
Step 8: Downweight src_1006 public tree because it lost historical jurisdictions.
Step 9: Produce corrected place objects with old_name, modern_name, old_jurisdiction and archive workflow.
Step 10: Generate research tasks by historical jurisdiction, not modern country alone.

place_I3_1924_Dnipropetrovsk.reason: GEDCOM uses Ekaterinoslav/Russian Empire label for Soviet-era 1924 event; corrected to Dnipropetrovsk region, Ukrainian SSR

corrected_place.historical_jurisdiction: German Empire, Province of West Prussia

mennonite_match_as_jewish_ancestor.reason: Mennonite regional DNA cluster remains separate from Jewish Levitin branch absent marriage/adoption/conversion evidence

true_place_mappings: Brest 1863 -> Brest-Litovsk/Brisk, Grodno Governorate, Russian Empire; modern Brest, Belarus
Koniuchy 1902 -> Congress Poland/Russian Empire, Hrubieszów-area routing; modern Lubelskie, Poland
Dnipropetrovsk 1924 -> Ukrainian SSR/Soviet-era context, not Russian Empire Ekaterinoslav
Danzig 1880 -> German Empire/West Prussia; modern Gdańsk, Poland
Molotschna 1845 -> Mennonite Settlement, Taurida Governorate, Russian Empire; modern Molochansk region, Ukraine
false_claims: Modern country alone determines archive routing.
Brest 1863 should use modern Belarus civil records only.
Molotschna does not need Mennonite colony workflow.
Mennonite DNA near Ekaterinoslav proves Jewish ancestor connection.

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must map Brest 1863 to Brest-Litovsk/Grodno Governorate, not modern Belarus-only routing.
Engine must map Koniuchy 1902 to Congress Poland/Russian Empire.
Engine must flag 1924 Ekaterinoslav/Russian Empire as period mismatch.
Engine must map Danzig 1880 to German Empire/West Prussia and modern Gdańsk, Poland.

expected.historical_jurisdiction: German Empire, Province of West Prussia

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must route Molotschna 1845 through Mennonite colony/Taurida workflow.
Engine must keep Mennonite Wiens/Friesen DNA cluster separate from Jewish Levitin branch unless bridge evidence appears.

created_for: AutoTreeGen Test Tree Corpus + Evaluation Suite
